artifact.content_preview:
De : pierre.gagnon@ssc-spc.gc.ca
À : alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc : marie.lefebvre@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Objet : Demande d'ajout — Modernisation des postes de travail des utilisateurs
Date : 8 mars 2026

Bonjour Alice,

Je vous écris pour soumettre une demande d'ajout de portée au projet
NGIS Phase 3 de la part du gestionnaire de prestation de services.

Dans le cadre de la consolidation des sites B et C, plusieurs employés
des centres de données affectés devront être relocalisés au site A à
Gatineau. Le

extracted_tasks.text: Examiner la demande d'ajout de portée et la soumettre au comité directeur
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Pourriez-vous examiner cette demande et la soumettre au comité directeur lors de la prochaine réunion du 10 mars ?

extracted_tasks.text: Soumettre la demande d'ajout de portée au comité directeur
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: soumettre au comité directeur lors de la prochaine réunion du 10 mars

extracted_tasks.text: Décision : Inclure le remplacement des équipements dans le projet NGIS Phase 3
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Le gestionnaire de prestation propose que le projet NGIS Phase 3 inclue également le remplacement de leurs équipements de travail

extracted_tasks.text: Livraison et configuration des 85 ordinateurs portables et 40 postes fixes
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Délai : livraison et configuration avant le 15 avril 2026

alerts.detected_ask: Inclusion of the replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating to Site A, with an estimated cost of $210,000 - $240,000.
alerts.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)
alerts.suggested_action: Formally reject the request as a scope change requiring a Change Control Board (CCB) review and potential budget amendment, as it directly violates the 'Out of Scope' definition for end-user hardware refresh.

llm_calls.content:
{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B (Ottawa) and C (Hull) physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A (Gatineau)",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",
    "Network performance baseline testing post-migration",
    "Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A",
    "Active Directory schema extension and forest restructuring",
    "Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing",
    "Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD",
    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",
    "Monthly steering committee presentations",
    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Project-level change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming (code changes, architecture changes, hosting platform changes)",
    "Migration of workloads to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud)",
    "Departmental end-user training programs",
    "Procurement of new application licenses resulting from platform migration",
    "Upgrades to the network operations centre (NOC) monitoring platform"
  ],
  "deliverables": [
    "Project Management Plan (Due: October 31, 2024)",
    "Technical Design Document (TDD) (Due: December 15, 2024)",
    "Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package (Due: January 31, 2025)",
    "Migration Runbook for Sites B and C (Due: February 28, 2025)",
    "User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan (Due: March 10, 2026)",
    "Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) (Due: April 30, 2026)",
    "Post-Migration Validation Report (Due: May 31, 2026)",
    "Project Closure Report (Due: June 30, 2026)"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget cap of $2,400,000 inclusive of all fees and taxes",
    "Budget variance exceeding 15% requires formal amendment and Treasury Board submission",
    "Go-Live deadline no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date no later than June 30, 2026",
    "Schedule extensions require ADM-level approval",
    "Compliance with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management",
    "Compliance with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "Compliance with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "Compliance with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "Compliance with Official Languages Act for client-facing deliverables",
    "Scope changes require written approval from the Project Sponsor and impact assessment"
  ]
}

Project-level change management and stakeholder communications

Migration of workloads to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud)

Upgrades to the network operations centre (NOC) monitoring platform

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.2,
  "drift_type": "new_requirement",
  "detected_ask": "Inclusion of the replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating to Site A, with an estimated cost of $210,000 - $240,000.",
  "baseline_reference": "OUT OF SCOPE: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)",
  "suggested_action": "Formally reject the request as a scope change requiring a Change Control Board (CCB) review and potential budget amendment, as it directly violates the 'Out of Scope' definition for end-user hardware refresh.",
  "reasoning": "The document explicitly requests the procurement and replacement of laptops and desktops. The project baseline clearly categorizes 'End-user device refresh or replacement' as OUT OF SCOPE. While the request argues for operational efficiency, it introduces a new category of deliverables (hardware procurement) that is not covered by the current budget cap or technical scope, constituting a clear scope drift."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Inclusion of the replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating to Site A, with an estimated cost of $210,000 - $240,000.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)
parsed_json.suggested_action: Formally reject the request as a scope change requiring a Change Control Board (CCB) review and potential budget amendment, as it directly violates the 'Out of Scope' definition for end-user hardware refresh.
parsed_json.reasoning: The document explicitly requests the procurement and replacement of laptops and desktops. The project baseline clearly categorizes 'End-user device refresh or replacement' as OUT OF SCOPE. While the request argues for operational efficiency, it introduces a new category of deliverables (hardware procurement) that is not covered by the current budget cap or technical scope, constituting a clear scope drift.
